functions.name: automate.ai_suggest_helper
functions.description: Generate an AI-suggested reply message for the latest customer conversation.

args.userid: User ID of the current agent (string or integer)
args.currentmsg: The most recent message text from the customer (string)
args.conversation_id: Unique conversation ID for the current email thread (string)

returns.ai_reply: The AI-generated suggested reply text. Returns null if no reply is generated.

functions.notes: Retrieves previous messages for the given conversation and uses the AI model to generate a contextual reply suggestion.

functions.name: automate.helper_make_reply_email
functions.description: Automatically compose and send an AI-generated reply to the latest inbound email in a conversation.

args.userid: User ID of the agent (string or integer)
args.from_email: Agent's email address used for sending (string)

returns.status: True if email successfully sent, False if failed, or null on exception
returns.sent_value: Result or metadata from send_pilot_messages() if the email was sent successfully

functions.notes: Fetches the latest inbound email from the user's conversations, generates an AI reply using suggest_helper_base, and sends it automatically using send_pilot_messages.

functions.name: automate.get_conversation_history
functions.description: Fetch the sorted email conversation history for a user.

args.userid: User ID of the agent (string or integer)
args.clientid: Client ID or connected Gmail account ID (string)

returns.conversations: List of conversation objects sorted by timestamp. Each includes message list and metadata.

functions.notes: Supports helper_make_reply_email by providing structured conversation data for message analysis.

functions.name: automate.send_ai_reply
functions.description: Send a finalized AI-generated email reply using the Gmail channel.

args.client_id: Client ID of the connected Gmail account (string)
args.subject: Email subject (string)
args.is_reply: Boolean flag, true if replying to an existing thread

returns.status: True if sent successfully, False if failed
returns.response_metadata: Metadata or message ID returned by Gmail service

functions.notes: Wrapper for send_pilot_messages() specific to sending AI-generated replies. Called internally by helper_make_reply_email.

functions.name: automate.fetch_autopilot
functions.description: Fetch the current AutoPilot configuration and metadata for a specific user.

args.user_id: User ID whose AutoPilot configuration should be fetched (string or integer)

returns.autopilot_config: The stored AutoPilot configuration JSON or null if not found

functions.notes: Retrieves AutoPilot setup including modes, triggers, and active workflows from the database.

functions.name: automate.persist_autopilot
functions.description: Persist or update AutoPilot configuration data for a user.

args.user_id: User ID of the current user (string or integer)
args.autopilot_data: JSON object containing the AutoPilot configuration details

returns.status: True if successfully persisted, False otherwise

functions.notes: Used to create or update AutoPilot configuration in persistent storage (e.g., RDS or S3).

functions.name: automate.suggest_reply
functions.description: Generate a suggested AI reply for an ongoing conversation, without sending it.

args.user_id: User ID of the agent (string or integer)
args.message_text: The last message text from the customer (string)

returns.ai_suggestion: AI-generated suggested reply text, or null if no reply was produced

functions.notes: Invokes the internal AI model to produce a context-aware reply without sending. Useful for preview or manual mode.

functions.name: automate.auto_reply_email
functions.description: Automatically send an AI-generated email reply using AutoPilot mode.

args.user_id: User ID of the agent (string or integer)
args.client_id: Client ID of connected Gmail account (string)
args.subject: Email subject line (string)

returns.status: True if reply successfully sent, False if failed

functions.notes: Combines suggest_reply and send_ai_reply to automatically generate and send replies for active conversations.

functions.name: automate.activate_autopilot
functions.description: Activate AutoPilot for the current user, enabling automated email replies and workflows.

args.user_id: User ID to activate AutoPilot for (string or integer)

returns.status: True if successfully activated, False if failed

functions.notes: Enables automated reply handling and scheduled monitoring of incoming emails for the specified user.

functions.name: automate.revoke_autopilot
functions.description: Revoke or disable AutoPilot mode for a user, halting all automation actions.

args.user_id: User ID to disable AutoPilot for (string or integer)

returns.status: True if AutoPilot successfully revoked, False otherwise

functions.notes: Used to pause or completely stop AutoPilot functionality for a given account.

functions.name: automate.change_mode
functions.description: Switch AutoPilot mode (e.g., from manual to auto or semi-auto) for the user.

args.mode: New mode to set, such as 'auto', 'manual', or 'semi-auto' (string)

returns.status: True if mode successfully changed, False otherwise

functions.notes: Updates AutoPilot mode dynamically, allowing users to toggle automation preferences.

functions.name: automate.reset_autopilot
functions.description: Reset AutoPilot configuration to default settings for a specific user.

args.user_id: User ID whose AutoPilot configuration should be reset (string or integer)

returns.status: True if reset successfully, False otherwise

functions.notes: Clears existing automation rules, triggers, and modes, restoring the default AutoPilot state.

functions.name: automate.create_custom_email_body
functions.description: creates custom message body based on user requirements

args.user_input: custom user input for email body

functions.notes: uses ai to create email body also uses users business info if needed

functions.name: automate.generate_file_from_ai
functions.description: Generates a file (txt, md, css, html, docx, pptx) with content based on user input. Filename and file type are dynamically suggested by AI.

args.user_input: The description or instructions for the file content (string). Can specify style, format, structure, or type of file.

returns.file_path: The path to the generated file on disk (string).

functions.notes: Uses the AI model to generate both content and a suitable filename with extension. Supports structured content for Word (.docx) and PowerPoint (.pptx) files.
